title: Duckdb throws error for querying group for interval type
description:
### What happens?

I ran a query for grouping with interval type, duckdb will throw an error

> ExpressionExecutor::Execute called with a result vector of type VARCHAR that does not match expression type INTERVAL

### To Reproduce

I ran following queries and got this error
```
CREATE TABLE interval_tbl (__dd__4d2_rowid INT, id INT, interval_col INTERVAL);
INSERT INTO interval_tbl VALUES (0, 1, INTERVAL 1 SECONDS);

SELECT interval_col FROM (SELECT * from interval_tbl) GROUP BY ROLLUP(interval_col);
```

### OS:

MacOs

### DuckDB Version:

1.2.2

### DuckDB Client:

C++

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Tuyen Nguyen Ngoc

### Affiliation:

Ihands Company

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have not tested with any build

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

No - Other reason (please specify in the issue body)

- [ ] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue?

- [ ] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE interval_test (id INT, interval_col INTERVAL, value_col FLOAT);
INSERT INTO interval_test VALUES (1, INTERVAL '5 MINUTES', 10.5), (2, INTERVAL '10 MINUTES', 20.0);
SELECT interval_col, SUM(value_col) FROM interval_test GROUP BY interval_col WITH ROLLUP;
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INT, description VARCHAR, interval_col INTERVAL NOT NULL);
INSERT INTO another_table VALUES (1, 'Test', INTERVAL '1 HOUR'), (2, 'Sample', INTERVAL '2 HOURS');
key_bug_features: Grouping query with interval type causes type mismatch error
